description_long: This research institute building features a long rectangular main structure with a prominent cylindrical section on one end, which is composed of stacked, horizontal bands. The facade is characterized by repetitive, evenly spaced window lines and a flat roof, suggesting a utilitarian and functional design. The exterior appears to be constructed primarily from light-colored concrete or stone, giving it a clean, institutional look. The base of the building is set on a dark platform, possibly representing a paved area or foundation. There are no visible decorative elements, signage, or landscaping details, emphasizing a focus on practicality. The overall form and massing suggest a modernist architectural influence, with an emphasis on geometric shapes and minimal ornamentation.
description: A large, modernist research institute with a rectangular main block and a cylindrical wing, clad in light-colored material and featuring repetitive window bands.
description_view_0: The front and side of the building are visible, highlighting the cylindrical section with horizontal bands and the long, windowed facade.
description_view_1: The cylindrical end is prominent, with the flat, unadorned rear wall and the length of the building receding into the background.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the building is shown, again emphasizing the cylindrical section and the extended, windowed facade.
description_view_3: The rear of the building is visible, showing a plain, flat wall and the length of the roof, with the cylindrical section at the far end.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is not clearly visible in any view, but the facade suggests a likely ground-level entrance integrated into the long, windowed section, possibly with double glass doors and minimal decorative elements.
